title: Delete unused story_history config block + DEFAULT_STORY_HISTORY_* constants
description:
config.py:93-98 has a story_history block (storage_path, database_path, recent_items_limit) that is unused scaffolding. Remove it along with DEFAULT_STORY_HISTORY_PATH and DEFAULT_STORY_HISTORY_DB_PATH constants. Also update config.yaml if it surfaces these defaults.

This task is independent of the rest of the persistence work and can land any time.
close_reason: Removed DEFAULT_STORY_HISTORY_PATH, DEFAULT_STORY_HISTORY_DB_PATH constants and story_history block from DEFAULT_CONFIG and _ensure_default_paths_exist.

title: Pod: persistence/ package — typed config, errors, httpx client using protocol DTOs
description:
Create the pod-side persistence client package.

Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence/__init__.py — public exports: PersistenceClient, get_client(), is_enabled()

Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence/config.py — pydantic-settings BaseSettings:
- METADATA_API_URL: AnyHttpUrl | None
- METADATA_API_KEY: SecretStr | None
- METADATA_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: float = 30
- METADATA_MAX_RETRIES: int = 5
- is_enabled() returns True iff URL and KEY are both set

Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence/errors.py — typed exception hierarchy:
- PersistenceError (base)
- TransientPersistenceError (retryable: 5xx, network, timeout)
- PermanentPersistenceError (4xx other than 429)

Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence/client.py — PersistenceClient wrapping httpx.AsyncClient:
- All methods use protocol DTOs from creepy_pasta_protocol — NEVER redefine wire schemas
- Methods: create_or_get_script, upsert_voice, create_run, patch_run, create_chunks, upload_chunk_audio, upload_final_audio, list_runs, get_run, stream_audio
- Each method retries via tenacity on TransientPersistenceError with exponential backoff (1s base, 60s cap, max 5 attempts) — but the OUTBOX is the real durability story; this is just an inner loop
- Bearer auth header from config
- Logs at INFO on success, WARNING on transient, ERROR on permanent
- Sets a User-Agent so home server logs are clear

mypy strict. No dict[str, Any] in public surface.
close_reason: Created persistence/{__init__,config,errors,client}.py. PersistenceClient wraps httpx.AsyncClient with tenacity retry (module-level _with_retry decorator), Bearer auth, User-Agent. All 10 methods implemented. Added httpx/tenacity/pydantic/pydantic-settings to lite_runpod/requirements.txt. mypy strict: 4 files clean.

title: Pod: make execute_lite_clone_run async; switch job worker to asyncio task
description:
Refactor the synthesis entrypoint to be async so it can call the persistence client cleanly.

Changes:
- run_orchestrator.py:223 execute_lite_clone_run -> async def
- The synth body itself is mostly CPU-bound; wrap engine.synthesize() and other CPU-heavy calls in asyncio.to_thread() to keep the event loop responsive
- run_orchestrator.py:437 run_lite_clone_job -> still callable from threading.Thread for now, but its body becomes:
    asyncio.run(_run_lite_clone_job_async(job_id, request))
  with the async impl doing the awaits.
  Alternatively (cleaner): switch /api/jobs (routes.py:127) to spawn an asyncio.create_task on the running event loop instead of a thread. Pick this if it doesn't break the existing job_store.update progress callbacks.
- routes.py:124 /tts is already async — only needs `await` added.
- All progress_callback invocations should be wrapped so they don't block the event loop unnecessarily.

Acceptance:
- python -m py_compile across all modules still passes
- Existing /tts and /api/jobs tests (manual via UI) still produce the same audio
- No persistence calls yet — those land in the next bead

This is a pure refactor. Land it before adding persistence integration so any regressions are isolated.
close_reason: execute_lite_clone_run is now async; CPU-heavy calls wrapped in asyncio.to_thread(); run_lite_clone_job is async; /api/jobs uses asyncio.create_task(); /tts awaits the async function.

title: Pod: sqlite outbox + drain task with tenacity retries
description:
Add the outbox layer that makes persistence resilient to network blips.

Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence/outbox.py:
- SQLite file at outputs/persistence_outbox.sqlite (uses aiosqlite)
- Single table: outbox(id integer pk, kind text, payload_json text, blob_path text nullable, attempts int default 0, last_error text nullable, created_at text, next_attempt_at text)
- Kinds: 'create_script' | 'create_run' | 'patch_run' | 'create_chunks' | 'upload_chunk_audio' | 'upload_final_audio'
- Each kind has a typed payload schema (Pydantic models in outbox.py — these are internal so they live next to the outbox, not in creepy_pasta_protocol)
- For audio uploads, payload_json holds metadata + blob_path points at a temp file under outputs/persistence_outbox/ that holds the encoded audio bytes
- enqueue(kind, payload, blob_bytes=None) -> int
- drain_once(client) -> int (drains all rows whose next_attempt_at is in the past, returns count attempted)
- background_drain_loop(client, interval=5.0) -> never returns; runs in an asyncio task
- exponential backoff: next_attempt_at = now + min(60, 2 ** attempts) seconds on failure
- on success: delete the outbox row + the blob file
- on permanent failure: leave the row with last_error, log WARNING; manual intervention to clear

PersistenceClient gains an enqueue_or_send mode: if METADATA_API_URL is reachable, send directly; on TransientPersistenceError, fall back to enqueue. (Or always enqueue + drain — simpler and more uniform. Pick the always-enqueue path for v1.)

Lifecycle:
- Drain runs once on startup (drain whatever the previous pod left behind)
- Then the background loop runs every 5s
- On shutdown, the loop is cancelled cleanly

mypy strict.
close_reason: Implemented persistence/outbox.py: SQLite outbox with aiosqlite, all 6 kind constants, typed internal payload schemas, enqueue/enqueue_*/drain_once/background_drain_loop, exponential back-off, blob file management, causal ORDER BY. Added aiosqlite to requirements. Exported Outbox from persistence/__init__.py.

title: metadata-server: converters.py (ORM ↔ DTO) + service layer skeleton
description:
Build the conversion seam between ORM and wire DTOs, and stub out the service layer.

app/converters.py — the ONLY place ORM and wire DTOs touch:
- script_to_dto(orm: Script) -> ScriptDTO
- voice_to_dto(orm: Voice) -> VoiceDTO
- audio_blob_to_dto(orm: AudioBlob) -> AudioBlobDTO
- chunk_to_dto(orm: Chunk) -> ChunkDTO
- run_to_summary(orm: Run) -> RunSummaryDTO
- run_to_detail(orm: Run) -> RunDetailDTO  (includes chunks + final audio)
- create_run_request_to_orm_kwargs(req: CreateRunRequest) -> dict[str, Any]  (the only allowed Any in the codebase, marked with a comment)
- patch_run_request_apply(orm: Run, req: PatchRunRequest) -> None  (mutates the ORM with only set fields)

app/services/__init__.py
app/services/scripts.py — async create_or_get(session, text) -> Script
app/services/voices.py — async upsert(session, filename, audio_blob) -> Voice
app/services/runs.py — async create(session, req), patch(session, run_id, req), get_summary_list(session, limit, offset), get_detail(session, run_id)
app/services/chunks.py — async bulk_upsert(session, run_id, specs)
app/services/audio.py — async store_chunk_audio(session, run_id, chunk_index, bytes, metadata), store_final_audio(session, run_id, bytes, metadata), get_blob_for_streaming(session, audio_blob_id)

Services take typed args, return typed results, never import FastAPI.
close_reason: Created app/converters.py (all 8 converter functions) and app/services/{__init__,scripts,voices,runs,chunks,audio}.py. Also fixed scaffold bug: AudioStore.stream Protocol signature changed from async def to def (async generators are sync callables returning AsyncGenerator). mypy strict: 17 files clean.

title: metadata-server: audio upload endpoints + /v1/audio/{id} streaming + LocalFilesystemAudioStore
description:
Implement the audio upload + streaming endpoints. This finishes the metadata-svc API surface.

app/storage.py LocalFilesystemAudioStore (full impl):
- put(bytes, ext) -> writes to AUDIO_STORAGE_ROOT/{yyyy}/{mm}/{dd}/{audio_blob_id}.{ext}, computes sha256 + byte_size, returns (storage_key, sha256, byte_size)
- stream(storage_key) -> AsyncIterator[bytes] using aiofiles
- Atomic write (write to .tmp then rename)

app/routes/audio.py:
- POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/chunks/{chunk_index}/audio
  - multipart: audio file + UploadChunkAudioMetadata (format, sample_rate, duration_sec, attempts_used, validation_json)
  - response_model=AudioBlobDTO
  - Stores via AudioStore, creates audio_blobs row, links to chunks row
- POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/final_audio
  - multipart: audio file + UploadFinalAudioMetadata
  - response_model=AudioBlobDTO
  - Stores via AudioStore, creates audio_blobs row, sets runs.final_audio_id
- GET /v1/audio/{audio_blob_id}
  - Looks up audio_blobs row, returns FileResponse (or StreamingResponse) with the right Content-Type and Content-Length

All multipart parsing uses Pydantic for the metadata sidecar — pull metadata fields as a separate Form() group typed via the wire DTOs.

Keep audio bytes out of memory where possible — stream multipart uploads to disk.
close_reason: Created app/routes/audio.py (POST chunk audio, POST final audio, GET /v1/audio/{id} streaming). Updated storage.py: atomic writes via .tmp→rename, aiofiles streaming, added get_audio_store/set_audio_store. Added aiofiles + types-aiofiles to pyproject.toml. mypy strict: 22 files clean.

title: Epic: LLM text normalization for dates and dotted identifiers (PR #2)
description:
Rewrite problematic tokens (dates, dotted identifiers, abbreviations) in the source text before the chunker runs, so Chatterbox reads them naturally. Uses a small local LLM (Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct) with disk caching keyed on source text hash. Runs once per unique document; cached re-runs pay near-zero cost.

**Problems observed on `input_longer.txt`**
1. `07-02-2017` is read literally as digits ("zero seven zero two...") instead of as a spoken date.
2. `Left.Right.AS` (a zip filename in the story) is read with unwanted pauses because Chatterbox treats each `.` as a sentence terminator.

**Why LLM, not regex**
Regex can handle common date formats but struggles with ambiguous dotted identifiers — `Left.Right.AS` must be flattened, but `U.S.`, `Mr.`, `e.g.` must be preserved. A small LLM uses context to decide correctly; regex requires a rule per edge case.

**Why preprocessing, not chunker surgery**
Even if the chunker keeps `Left.Right.AS` in one chunk, Chatterbox's own text frontend still reads periods as pauses. The only complete fix is to rewrite the text before TTS sees it.

**Cost on RTX 3090**
Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct runs at ~50-80 tok/s locally. A typical story (~1000 tok in + ~1200 tok out) takes ~25-35s once, then cached forever keyed on `sha256(text + prompt_version + model_id)`. VRAM ~3GB alongside Chatterbox's ~5-8GB, fits comfortably in 24GB.

Feature-flagged with `enable_text_normalization`; ships as `false` by default, A/B tested, then flipped to `true`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `07-02-2017` in source text produces a naturally-spoken date in the generated audio.
- `Left.Right.AS` is read as one continuous utterance without interior pauses.
- Cache hit rate = 100% on re-runs of the same source text (second run normalizes in <100ms).
- LLM fallback (empty output, truncation, length drift) returns raw text and logs a warning — never breaks a run.
- Normalization changes are persisted in the manifest and visible in the lite_ui chunk previews.
- Wall-clock overhead on first run: < 45s. On cached re-runs: < 3s.
close_reason: Implemented: normalize_text_with_llm() in utils.py, normalization step in execute_lite_clone_run(), enable_text_normalization feature flag, LiteCloneRunResponse.normalized_text field.

title: Pod: Twingate headless client in Dockerfile + entrypoint wait + new env vars
description:
Get the GPU pod onto the Twingate network at boot.

lite_runpod/Dockerfile:
- Add `RUN curl -fsSL https://binaries.twingate.com/client/linux/install.sh | bash` (verify the install method against current Twingate docs at task time)
- COPY ../creepy_pasta_protocol /tmp/creepy_pasta_protocol then `pip install -e /tmp/creepy_pasta_protocol`
- COPY persistence /app/persistence

lite_runpod/docker-entrypoint.sh:
- Add a function `start_twingate` that:
  - Writes $TWINGATE_SERVICE_KEY (JSON) to a file
  - Starts the headless client in the background
  - Polls DNS resolution (or `curl --max-time 2 $METADATA_API_URL/healthz`) up to 30s
  - On success: log 'Twingate tunnel up'
  - On timeout: log a WARNING and continue (persistence will simply enqueue everything to the outbox)
- Call start_twingate before exec'ing the FastAPI server (only if TWINGATE_SERVICE_KEY is set)
- mkdir -p $APP_DIR/outputs/persistence_outbox

New env vars (add to RunPod template):
- TWINGATE_NETWORK
- TWINGATE_SERVICE_KEY (JSON content; mount as a Docker secret if RunPod supports it)
- METADATA_API_URL
- METADATA_API_KEY

config.yaml: no changes — these are env-only.
close_reason: Twingate headless client install added to Dockerfile; start_twingate() function added to entrypoint with 30s poll; persistence_outbox dir created at startup; build context changed to creepy_pasta/ parent; persistence/ and creepy_pasta_protocol/ COPYed into image; new env vars documented.

title: Postgres-backed persistence for Chatterbox TTS via Twingate metadata server
description:
Wire the RunPod GPU pod to a Postgres-backed metadata server on Shubham's home server, accessed over Twingate. Every script, chunk, per-chunk audio, and final stitched audio gets persisted on the home server. Pod stays stateless; home server is source of truth.

Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/fizzy-floating-simon.md
Mirror checklist: workspace root TODO.md

Three new components in this workspace:
- creepy_pasta_protocol/ — shared Pydantic v2 wire DTOs (single source of truth for the wire format)
- metadata-server/ — new FastAPI service on the home server (PG + LocalFilesystemAudioStore + Twingate Connector)
- Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence/ — typed httpx client + sqlite outbox on the pod side

Discipline: strict Pydantic v2 + SQLAlchemy 2 typed ORM throughout. PydanticJSONB TypeDecorator so JSONB columns are typed end-to-end. mypy strict on all new packages.

Out of scope: the future orchestrator server, MinIO/S3 migration, multi-user auth.

title: lite_ui history panel + audio playback through proxy (follow-up, not blocking MVP)

Add a History tab to the lite UI that surfaces past runs from the metadata server.

Scope:

- New JS in lite_ui/script.js:
  - On tab open, fetch GET /api/history?limit=50
  - Render a list with: timestamp, run_label, voice filename, chunk count, duration_sec, status badge
  - Click a row to expand: fetch GET /api/history/{id}, render the chunks with per-chunk audio players using GET /api/history/audio/{audio_blob_id}, plus the final audio player
  - Add a "Reload from this run" button that copies the run's settings into the main form
- Pagination: simple Older/Newer buttons (offset increments by limit)
- Empty state: 'No runs yet — generate something to populate history'

Out of scope: search, filters, deletions. Add follow-up beads if needed.

Acceptance: from a Twingate-connected browser, the History tab shows real rows from the home-server PG and audio plays back inline.
close_reason: Completed: Added tab navigation, history panel with run list, detail view, audio playback, reload functionality, pagination, and empty/503 states

title: metadata-server: /v1/scripts and /v1/voices endpoints (routes + services)
description:
Implement the script and voice resource endpoints.

app/routes/scripts.py:
- POST /v1/scripts (body: CreateScriptRequest, response_model=ScriptDTO)
  - Hashes text with sha256
  - Idempotent: if a script with that sha256 exists, return existing row
  - Otherwise insert and return the new row

app/routes/voices.py:
- POST /v1/voices (multipart: filename + audio file, response_model=CreateVoiceResponse)
  - Idempotent on filename
  - Computes sha256 + duration of the audio file
  - Stores audio via the AudioStore singleton, creates audio_blobs row, then creates voices row pointing at it
- GET /v1/voices (response_model=list[VoiceDTO])

Both routes use the Bearer auth dependency from app/auth.py and the get_session dependency from app/db.py.

All handlers must set response_model so OpenAPI is accurate. No raw dicts in/out.
close_reason: Created app/routes/scripts.py (POST /v1/scripts create-or-get) and app/routes/voices.py (POST /v1/voices multipart upload, GET /v1/voices list). Added voices_svc.get_by_filename and list_all. mypy strict: 22 files clean.

title: Pod: integrate persistence calls into execute_lite_clone_run + run_lite_clone_job
description:
Wire the persistence client into the synthesis pipeline. All persistence calls go through enqueue (the outbox), so they NEVER block synthesis on a network round trip.

run_orchestrator.py:execute_lite_clone_run integration points (line numbers from current HEAD, recheck before editing):

1. After normalization (~line 246, after `synthesis_text = normalized`):
   `script_id = await persist.enqueue_create_script(synthesis_text)` — this returns immediately with a deterministic local id derived from sha256; the actual remote create happens via the outbox drain. The pod uses sha256 as the correlation key, the metadata server resolves it to a uuid on first sight.

   Important: scripts and runs need to be created BEFORE any chunk audio is uploaded. Either:
   (a) await the actual remote create (still cheap, single small POST), or
   (b) the outbox preserves causal ordering (kind ordering: create_script before create_run before create_chunks before upload_chunk_audio before upload_final_audio before patch_run-completed)
   
   Pick option (b) — the outbox enforces causal ordering by kind so we never block synthesis. Document this in outbox.py.

2. After build_chunk_preview + selected_entries (~line 251):
   `await persist.enqueue_create_run(script_sha256, voice_filename, settings_snapshot, run_label, output_format, source_chunk_count, selected_chunk_indices, pod_run_id=run_id_str)`
   `await persist.enqueue_create_chunks(pod_run_id, selected_entries)`

3. Before chunk loop:
   `await persist.enqueue_patch_run(pod_run_id, status='running', started_at=now())`

4. Inside chunk loop after `raw_chunks.append(best_audio)` (~line 346):
   - encode chunk audio: `chunk_bytes = encode_audio(best_audio, chunk_sr, settings.output_format, settings.target_sample_rate)`
   - `await persist.enqueue_upload_chunk_audio(pod_run_id, chunk_idx, chunk_bytes, settings.output_format, sample_rate, duration_sec, attempts_used, validation_result)`

5. After post_process_final_audio (~line 383):
   - encode: `final_bytes = encode_audio(final_audio, engine_sr, settings.output_format, settings.target_sample_rate)`
   - `await persist.enqueue_upload_final_audio(pod_run_id, final_bytes, ...)`

6. End of execute_lite_clone_run (just before return):
   `await persist.enqueue_patch_run(pod_run_id, status='completed', completed_at=now(), warnings=warnings, normalized_text=normalized_text)`

7. run_lite_clone_job exception branches (~line 454):
   `await persist.enqueue_patch_run(pod_run_id, status='failed', error=msg, completed_at=now())`

8. Manifest enrichment: when manifest.json is written (`_write_manifest`), include the pod_run_id and a `metadata_run_id` field if the create_run has been confirmed.

When persistence.is_enabled() is False, all enqueue_* calls are no-ops. The orchestrator works exactly as it does today.

The local outputs/lite_clone_runs/<run_id>/ writes stay UNCHANGED — this is the safety net.

mypy strict on every new line.
close_reason: All 8 enqueue_* integration points wired into execute_lite_clone_run; outbox lifecycle (open, drain_once, background_drain_loop) wired into app.py lifespan; persistence/__init__.py gains get_outbox/set_outbox singleton; FAILED patch enqueued on exception.

title: Stand up metadata-server on home server behind Twingate
description:
Deploy metadata-server to Shubham's home server. This is mostly an ops task.

Steps:
1. Provision a directory for the audio storage volume (e.g. /var/lib/creepy_pasta/audio) with appropriate ownership
2. Provision a Postgres data volume directory
3. Copy metadata-server/ + creepy_pasta_protocol/ to the home server (or git pull)
4. Fill in .env from .env.example with a strong METADATA_API_KEY, a DATABASE_URL, AUDIO_STORAGE_ROOT
5. docker compose up -d (postgres + metadata-svc)
6. docker compose exec metadata-svc alembic upgrade head
7. Confirm GET /healthz works on LAN: curl http://localhost:8080/healthz
8. Set up Twingate Connector container on the home server pointing at the LAN address of metadata-svc
9. Add a Twingate Resource for metadata-svc (e.g. metadata-svc.local with port 8080)
10. From a laptop on Twingate, hit /healthz through the resource hostname — confirm 200 + protocol_version
11. Document the operator commands in metadata-server/README.md (start, stop, alembic upgrade, backup PG dump, backup audio dir)
12. Confirm Postgres + the audio dir are NOT exposed to the public internet (only via Twingate)

Acceptance: a Twingate-connected client gets 200 OK from /healthz and POST /v1/scripts works end-to-end with the API key.

title: Pod: history proxy routes (/api/history, /api/history/{id}, /api/history/audio/{id})
description:
Add browser-facing history endpoints on the pod that proxy to metadata-svc. The browser only ever talks to the pod, never directly to the home server.

New file: Chatterbox-TTS-Server/routes_history.py (or a section of routes.py — pick the file that keeps routes.py readable, prefer a separate module).

Endpoints (all set response_model with the protocol DTOs):
- GET /api/history?limit=50&offset=0 -> list[RunSummaryDTO]
  - Calls PersistenceClient.list_runs(limit, offset)
- GET /api/history/{run_id} -> RunDetailDTO
  - Calls PersistenceClient.get_run(run_id)
- GET /api/history/audio/{audio_blob_id} -> StreamingResponse
  - Calls PersistenceClient.stream_audio(audio_blob_id)
  - Forwards Content-Type from the upstream response

If persistence is disabled (no METADATA_API_URL), these endpoints return 503 with a clear message.

No auth on the proxy endpoints for now (the pod itself is the auth boundary the user trusts). Add a TODO note.

Wire the new router into app.py alongside the existing router.
close_reason: routes_history.py created with GET /api/history, /api/history/audio/{blob_id}, /api/history/{run_id}; 503 when persistence disabled; wired into app.py; audio route registered before {run_id} to avoid capture.

title: Bootstrap creepy_pasta_protocol shared package (Pydantic v2 wire DTOs, enums, PROTOCOL_VERSION)
description:
Create a new pure-python package at workspace root: creepy_pasta_protocol/. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for wire-format DTOs that cross the Twingate boundary. Both metadata-server and Chatterbox-TTS-Server/persistence depend on it.

Layout:
- pyproject.toml (name='creepy-pasta-protocol', deps: pydantic>=2)
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/__init__.py — public re-exports + PROTOCOL_VERSION = '1'
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/common.py — Frozen base class with model_config(extra='forbid', frozen=True, str_strip_whitespace=True, populate_by_name=True); StrEnum subclasses for StorageBackend, RunStatus, AudioFormat
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/settings.py — ResolvedSettingsSnapshot mirroring run_orchestrator.ResolvedSettings
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/validation.py — ChunkValidationSnapshot
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/scripts.py — CreateScriptRequest, ScriptDTO
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/voices.py — VoiceDTO, CreateVoiceResponse
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/runs.py — CreateRunRequest, PatchRunRequest, RunSummaryDTO, RunDetailDTO, RunWarnings
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/chunks.py — ChunkSpec, ChunkDTO
- src/creepy_pasta_protocol/audio.py — AudioBlobDTO, UploadChunkAudioMetadata, UploadFinalAudioMetadata
- tests/test_roundtrip.py — every DTO model_validate(model_dump()) round-trip

Rules:
- All DTOs inherit Frozen base; extra='forbid' is non-negotiable
- No HTTP/SQLAlchemy/FastAPI imports — pure schemas + enums only
- mypy strict clean
close_reason: creepy_pasta_protocol package created at workspace root with all DTOs, enums, Frozen base, PROTOCOL_VERSION=1, and 17 passing round-trip tests.

title: Scaffold metadata-server FastAPI app (config, db, auth, storage, Dockerfile, compose, mypy strict)
description:
Create metadata-server/ at workspace root with the layout from the plan.

Files:
- pyproject.toml — fastapi, sqlalchemy[asyncio], asyncpg, alembic, pydantic, pydantic-settings, creepy-pasta-protocol (path dep ../creepy_pasta_protocol), uvicorn[standard], python-multipart, pytest, mypy, ruff
- mypy.ini — strict = True; warn_unused_ignores; no_implicit_optional

- app/config.py — pydantic-settings BaseSettings reading DATABASE_URL, METADATA_API_KEY, AUDIO_STORAGE_ROOT, LISTEN_HOST, LISTEN_PORT
- app/db.py — async create_async_engine + async_sessionmaker; Base = DeclarativeBase; get_session FastAPI dependency

- app/storage.py — AudioStore Protocol with put(bytes, ext) -> (storage_key, sha256, byte_size) and stream(storage_key) -> AsyncIterator[bytes]; LocalFilesystemAudioStore writes to AUDIO_STORAGE_ROOT/{yyyy}/{mm}/{dd}/{uuid}.{ext}
- app/main.py — FastAPI factory + lifespan that creates the AudioStore singleton; includes routes (placeholders for now)
- app/routes/health.py — GET /healthz returns {protocol_version: '1', status: 'ok'}; GET /readyz pings DB
- Dockerfile — python:3.11-slim, COPY ../creepy_pasta_protocol, pip install -e it, then app
- docker-compose.yml — postgres:16 with named volume + metadata-svc + named volume for audio storage
- .env.example — DATABASE_URL, METADATA_API_KEY, AUDIO_STORAGE_ROOT
- README.md — operator notes (compose up, alembic upgrade head, Twingate Connector setup, backup strategy)
- TODO.md — slice of workspace TODO.md scoped to metadata-server tasks

The actual ORM models, services, and resource routes are landed in subsequent beads.
close_reason: metadata-server/ scaffolded: pyproject.toml, mypy.ini, alembic config, app/{config,db,auth,storage,types,models,main}.py, routes/health.py, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .env.example, README.md.

title: Design metadata-server Postgres schema + Alembic 0001 migration
description:
Define the Postgres schema for metadata-server. Tables (all with uuid PK from gen_random_uuid + created_at timestamptz):

- scripts(id, text, text_sha256 unique, char_count, created_at)
- voices(id, filename unique, audio_blob_id fk, duration_sec, created_at)
- runs(id, script_id fk, voice_id fk nullable, run_label, status [enum], settings jsonb, output_format [enum], source_chunk_count, selected_chunk_indices int[], normalized_text, warnings jsonb, error, final_audio_id fk nullable, created_at, started_at, completed_at, pod_run_id)
- chunks(id, run_id fk on delete cascade, chunk_index, text, audio_blob_id fk nullable, attempts_used, validation jsonb nullable, unique(run_id, chunk_index))
- audio_blobs(id, storage_backend [enum], storage_key, sha256 indexed, byte_size, mime_type, format [enum AudioFormat], sample_rate, duration_sec, created_at)

PG enums (RunStatus, AudioFormat, StorageBackend) match the StrEnums in creepy_pasta_protocol.common.

Migration 0001:
- enable pgcrypto extension
- create the four enum types
- create all five tables in the right FK order
- index audio_blobs.sha256
- index runs.created_at desc (for history listing)

Alembic config (alembic.ini + alembic/env.py) reads DATABASE_URL from env via app.config.
close_reason: Alembic 0001 migration created with pgcrypto, all 3 PG enum types, and all 5 tables in FK order with correct indexes.

title: Add mypy strict to quality-gate command for protocol/persistence/metadata-server
description:
The existing quality-gate (AGENTS.md and Chatterbox-TTS-Server/AGENTS.md) only runs python -m compileall and node --check. Extend it to enforce mypy strict on the new typed modules.

Steps:
1. Add mypy>=1.10 to lite_runpod/requirements.txt and metadata-server/pyproject.toml
2. Add mypy.ini at workspace root with:
     [mypy]
     strict = True
     warn_unused_ignores = True
     no_implicit_optional = True
     [mypy-creepy_pasta_protocol.*]
     [mypy-persistence.*]
     [mypy-app.*]
3. Update Chatterbox-TTS-Server/AGENTS.md commands section to include:
     python -m mypy persistence creepy_pasta_protocol/src
4. Update metadata-server/README.md commands section to include:
     mypy app
5. Make sure the existing chatterbox engine code is excluded — it's not in scope for strict typing.
6. The quality-gate must be runnable from the workspace root; pin module path overrides as needed.

Acceptance: running the documented commands produces zero mypy errors against the new packages.
close_reason: Completed: Added mypy.ini at workspace root, added mypy>=1.10 to requirements.txt, updated AGENTS.md with type-check command

title: metadata-server: /v1/runs CRUD + /v1/runs/{id}/chunks bulk create
description:
Implement run + chunk row management endpoints (no audio yet).

app/routes/runs.py:
- POST /v1/runs (body: CreateRunRequest, response_model=RunDetailDTO)
  - Required: script_id, voice_id (nullable), settings (ResolvedSettingsSnapshot), output_format, run_label, source_chunk_count, selected_chunk_indices, pod_run_id
  - Initial status = QUEUED
- PATCH /v1/runs/{run_id} (body: PatchRunRequest, response_model=RunDetailDTO)
  - Partial update: status, started_at, completed_at, error, warnings, normalized_text
  - Only sets fields explicitly present (uses model_dump(exclude_unset=True))
- GET /v1/runs?limit=&offset= (response_model=list[RunSummaryDTO]) ordered by created_at desc
- GET /v1/runs/{run_id} (response_model=RunDetailDTO) loads chunks + final_audio eagerly

app/routes/chunks.py:
- POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/chunks (body: list[ChunkSpec], response_model=list[ChunkDTO])
  - Bulk upsert on (run_id, chunk_index) — idempotent so the pod can retry safely
  - Initial audio_blob_id is null

All handlers via Bearer auth + get_session. Use the service layer functions, do not write SQL in route handlers.
close_reason: Created app/routes/runs.py (POST/PATCH/GET /v1/runs, GET /v1/runs/{id}) and app/routes/chunks.py (POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/chunks). All use service layer only. mypy strict: 22 files clean.

title: Epic: Chunk validation + retry loop (silence killer, PR #1)
description:
Detect silent, near-silent, and truncated TTS chunks right after synthesis and automatically regenerate them with seed variation, keeping the best-effort attempt when all retries fail. End goal: zero silent chunks in final audio.

**Problem observed**
Running `input_longer.txt` through the lite_ui clone pipeline (image `shubh67678/chatterbox-lite-runpod@sha256:110af513f5baa1e711fdbad04ed14922386425e967c4dd48e46ac603ad32f55c`) produced a completely silent chunk for the sentence starting "After months of fruitless internships and rejections, Alice called a flat meeting, telling us that she was moving to America, accepting a position chasing stories for National Public Radio." With the fixed seed=1234 in lite_runpod/config.yaml, re-running is deterministic and reproduces the failure.

**Shape**
Phase 1 = audio-level validator in utils.py (energy + peak + duration + voiced-ratio checks).
Phase 2 = retry loop in execute_lite_clone_run wrapping engine.synthesize with seed variation and a best-effort fallback.

Feature-flagged throughout; defaults preserve existing behavior until enabled in config.
acceptance_criteria:
- Re-running `input_longer.txt` end-to-end produces zero silent chunks across 3 consecutive runs with different seeds.
- Every chunk in the manifest has `validation.passed == true`, or is flagged in `warnings` with `attempts_used > 1`.
- No regression on runs that already worked (existing outputs in `outputs/lite_clone_runs/` still produce valid audio).
- Feature can be disabled via `enable_chunk_validation: false` and behavior matches pre-change.
close_reason: Implemented: validate_chunk_audio() in utils.py, retry loop in execute_lite_clone_run(), enable_chunk_validation feature flag, SavedChunkInfo.validation and attempts_used fields.

title: metadata-server: PydanticJSONB TypeDecorator + ORM models with typed JSONB columns
description:
Create the typed ORM layer.

app/types.py:
- PydanticJSONB TypeDecorator subclassing sqlalchemy.types.TypeDecorator with impl=JSONB and cache_ok=True
- __init__(model: type[BaseModel])
- process_bind_param: model.model_dump(mode='json')
- process_result_value: model.model_validate(value)
- Generic over the Pydantic model type; mypy strict friendly

app/models.py — SQLAlchemy 2.x typed ORM models:
- Base = DeclarativeBase
- All columns use Mapped[T] / mapped_column() — no legacy Column() calls
- Script, Voice, AudioBlob, Run, Chunk
- runs.settings: Mapped[ResolvedSettingsSnapshot] = mapped_column(PydanticJSONB(ResolvedSettingsSnapshot))
- runs.warnings: Mapped[RunWarnings] = mapped_column(PydanticJSONB(RunWarnings))
- chunks.validation: Mapped[ChunkValidationSnapshot | None] = mapped_column(PydanticJSONB(ChunkValidationSnapshot), nullable=True)
- runs.status, runs.output_format, audio_blobs.storage_backend, audio_blobs.format use SQLAlchemy Enum bound to the StrEnums in creepy_pasta_protocol.common
- Relationships: Run.script, Run.voice, Run.chunks (cascade delete-orphan), Run.final_audio, Chunk.audio_blob, Voice.audio_blob

mypy strict clean.
close_reason: app/types.py (PydanticJSONB TypeDecorator) and app/models.py (full SQLAlchemy 2.x ORM) were already implemented in scaffold. Added py.typed marker to creepy_pasta_protocol. mypy strict: clean.

title: End-to-end verification on RunPod + outbox resilience test
description:
Verify the full system before declaring the persistence MVP done.

Test plan:

1. Local dev sanity (no Twingate):
   - Start lite_clone_server.py with METADATA_API_URL unset
   - Generate a small TTS run via the UI
   - Confirm: server starts cleanly, generation succeeds, outputs/ has files, no persistence activity in logs

2. Local dev with mocked metadata-svc:
   - Spin up metadata-server via docker-compose locally
   - Set METADATA_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 + METADATA_API_KEY
   - Generate a small run via the UI
   - Query Postgres directly: confirm 1 script row, 1 run row with status=completed, N chunk rows each with audio_blob_id, 1 final audio_blob row, runs.final_audio_id set
   - Confirm files exist at AUDIO_STORAGE_ROOT/{yyyy}/{mm}/{dd}/{uuid}.{ext}
   - Confirm outputs/ also has files (safety net intact)
   - Hit GET /api/history and GET /api/history/<run_id> via curl, verify shape

3. Outbox resilience:
   - Start a 5-chunk generation
   - Mid-run, docker compose stop metadata-svc
   - Confirm: synthesis continues, outbox sqlite grows, logs show enqueue
   - docker compose start metadata-svc
   - Within ~30s, confirm all outbox rows drained, run reaches status=completed in PG, all chunks have audio_blob_ids

4. RunPod end-to-end:
   - docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64 -f lite_runpod/Dockerfile -t shubh67678/chatterbox-lite-runpod:latest --push .
   - Start a RunPod pod with the new template (TWINGATE_*, METADATA_API_URL, METADATA_API_KEY env vars set)
   - Pod logs show: Twingate tunnel up; outbox drain task started
   - Generate a real run via the UI from a Twingate-connected laptop browser
   - Confirm rows in PG on the home server
   - Hit /api/history/audio/<blob_id> from the browser and confirm playback

5. Schema migration upgrade path:
   - Drop the PG volume
   - alembic upgrade head from scratch — succeeds
   - Add a no-op alembic revision (e.g. add a comment to a column) — alembic upgrade head applies it

If any of these fail, file follow-up beads with discovered-from links to this one.
